channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: How to build custom reasoning agents with a fraction of the compute
item.description: Training AI reasoning models demands resources that most enterprise teams do not have. Engineering teams are often forced to choose between distilling knowledge from large, expensive models or relying on reinforcement learning techniques that provide sparse feedback.Researchers at JD.com and several academic institutions recently introduced a new training paradigm that sidesteps this dilemma. The technique, called Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards with Self-Distillation (RLSD), combines the reliable performance tracking of reinforcement learning with the granular feedback of self-...

item.title: OpenAI Really Wants Codex to Shut Up About Goblins
item.description: “Never talk about goblins, gremlins, raccoons, trolls, ogres, pigeons, or other animals or creatures unless it is absolutely and unambiguously relevant,” reads OpenAI’s coding agent instructions.

item.title: It’s primetime for conspiracy theorist video creators
item.description: In the days since this year's White House Correspondents' Dinner was cut short when shots were fired at the event, there has been a boom of conspiracy theory videos created by people who insist that the entire situation was a false flag operation. These kinds of theories are nothing new, but the way they're spreading […]

item.title: 'The connective tissue between your data, your people, and your goals': Google Cloud positions Gemini Enterprise as the one-stop shop for all your agentic affairs
item.description: Gemini Enterprise platform gets a fresh look at Google Cloud Next 2026, with new app experiences and agent builder.

item.title: Elon Musk appeared more petty than prepared
item.description: Today the first witness was sworn in in Musk v. Altman: Elon Musk. I was surprised by how flat he seemed. This is not the first time I've seen Musk in court. During his defamation suit, he turned on the charm and the jury responded by finding him not guilty. Today he looked adrift and […]

item.title: Dyson's latest vacuum cleaner empties itself, so you don't have to get your hands dirty
item.description: With the Dyson V10 Konical and its Auto-empty Dok, there's no need to worry about emptying a messy dust cup partway through cleaning.

item.title: How to watch The House of the Spirits online from anywhere — stream the Prime Video literary adaptation
item.description: Prime Video is giving Isabel Allende's best-known novel a much bigger canvas this time around. Watch The House of the Spirits online from anywhere.

item.title: Quordle hints and answers for Wednesday, April 29 (game #1556)
item.description: Looking for Quordle clues? We can help. Plus get the answers to Quordle today and past solutions.

item.title: NYT Connections hints and answers for Wednesday, April 29 (game #1053)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.

item.title: NYT Strands hints and answers for Wednesday, April 29 (game #787)
item.description: Looking for NYT Strands answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, including the spangram.

item.title: 'A significant threat': These tiny 'trained' robots can catch invisible plastic dust in your drinking water
item.description: Researchers developed magnetic nanorobots that actively capture nanoplastics in water, achieving strong lab results while facing major efficiency and scalability challenges.

item.title: Those vanity Trump passports are rage baiting you
item.description: The limited edition passports are only available in Washington D.C., but their impact will be much bigger. That’s by design. The State Department just revealed a first look at a line of limited-edition passports designed to commemorate the 250th anniversary of American independence. They feature a large image of President Trump’s face.
